sources.notes: Statement and hard constraints n, q up to 2000000.
Official editorial and comments used to confirm the O(n+q) invariant.
Explains Ferrers transformation and O(1) contribution updates.

annotation.summary: Same online monotone-matrix decision as F1, with very large total n and q.
annotation.constraints: sum n <= 2000000 and sum q <= 2000000; requires linear memory and time.
annotation.core_idea: Use the hard invariant directly: valid matrices are exactly those row/column-permutable into a Ferrers shape. Maintain the actual row-pair contribution and the canonical Ferrers contribution induced by column heights; equality is necessary and sufficient.
annotation.complexity: O(n+q) time and O(n) memory over each test batch.
tricks: column-height layers build canonical Ferrers row counts
equality of contribution sums certifies the chain-graph condition

solution_variants.name: linear-ferrers-invariant
solution_variants.summary: On inserting (r,c), update sr by the previous row count and sc by the previous number of columns at the new height; answer YES iff sr == sc.

tags.evidence: The forbidden alternating 2x2 pattern is exactly an induced 2K2 obstruction to a bipartite chain graph/Ferrers matrix.
tags.description: Bipartite graphs whose neighborhoods on one side are nested; equivalently Ferrers or monotone binary matrices with no induced 2K2.
tags.created_reason: The existing bipartite-graph tag is too broad for Ferrers/chain-matrix characterizations used in matrix and graph problems.

tags.evidence: The hard constraints are met by maintaining sr and sc with constant-time contribution deltas per inserted black cell.
The proof compares compact contribution fingerprints of actual row counts and canonical Ferrers row counts.
The proof constructs the canonical row/column-permuted Ferrers matrix, but the online algorithm is invariant maintenance.
